title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: US senator says Tesla benefited from govt support, Elon Musk replies
content: Elon Musk has refuted claims that his companies, Tesla and SpaceX, owe their success to government aid, stating such incentives represent less than 2% of their value. The debate ignited between Senators Mike Lee and Ed Markey over taxpayer contributions to Musk's wealth. Musk asserted that removing EV tax credits actually boosted Tesla's market share.

title: HIV+ woman among 4 held in MP sex racket
content: A disturbing sex racket in Bhind, Madhya Pradesh, has taken a grim turn with police discovering one of the two arrested women has been HIV-positive for three years. The operation, which involved blackmail and false rape threats, saw four individuals apprehended.

title: Government: No cough syrup sale without prescription
content: Cough syrups will now require a doctor's prescription as the Centre has removed them from a decades-old exemption. This move, following concerns over contaminated syrups, brings all formulations under regular regulatory oversight. The change aims to enhance accountability and public health safety in the distribution of these commonly used medicines.

title: Microsoft president Brad Smith has a message for students booing tech CEOs
content: Microsoft president Brad Smith has responded to graduating students who booed AI at commencement ceremonies this spring, calling the backlash a "powerful wake-up call for the tech sector." In a 3,000-word essay, Smith acknowledged the class of 2026 faces a "perfect storm" of AI automation and tech layoffs, yet urged graduates to adapt rather than fear AI. His optimistic message arrives as Sam Altman, Mustafa Suleyman, and Dario Amodei clash over AI's job impact.

title: Woman can't seek maintenance hike after husband's death: High Court
content: The Bombay High Court ruled that a divorced wife cannot seek increased maintenance after her husband's death, but can still claim decreed amounts and arrears from his estate. The court clarified that the right to maintenance is personal and lasts a lifetime, but seeking enhancement requires both parties to be alive.

title: Aldon Smith’s death mystery deepens as family probes possible CTE connection
content: Aldon Smith’s family has launched an investigation into the former NFL star’s sudden death at age 36. According to ESPN, his brain will be examined in Boston for CTE and other trauma-related damage. No official cause of death has been released. The case has renewed discussion about brain injuries in football and the long-term effects of repeated head trauma.

title: Connor McDavid and Lauren Kyle’s custom Edmonton mansion showcases luxury, style and modern living
content: Edmonton Oilers captain Connor McDavid’s lavish Edmonton home continues to get attention years after it was featured on Architectural Digest. The bespoke property was designed with wife Lauren Kyle and combines modern sophistication with features tailored to the athlete. The mansion reflects the lifestyle and taste of one of the NHL’s biggest stars, with everything from entertainment spaces to training areas.

title: Celebrity planner behind Jack Antonoff’s wedding could shape Taylor Swift’s big day
content: Speculation is rife about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s million dollar wedding. Rumors about their wedding point to Madison Square Garden and a July 3rd date. Celebrity wedding planner Mark Seed who is known for designing Jack Antonoff and Margaret Qualley’s big day, is reportedly set to transform MSG for the event. This adds another layer to the unfolding narrative.

title: Ignou question papers leaked on messaging app?
content: Ignou is investigating allegations of question papers being sold on Telegram for its June-July 2026 term-end exams, with touts reportedly charging Rs 199 per subject. Despite complaints and screenshots shared, the university claims no evidence of a leak and has not cancelled any papers.

title: 'Very important': India coach reveals how Rohit remains crucial to Gill
content: Ahead of the second ODI against Afghanistan, India spin bowling coach Sairaj Bahutule backed Rohit Sharma, calling him a "champion cricketer" and highlighting his influence on ODI captain Shubman Gill and the team. Bahutule also praised KL Rahul's versatility and form, saying the batter can adapt to any situation and remains a key member across formats.

title: Census 2027: 23 states, UTs complete first phase of houselisting; 8 more underway
content: Houslisting operations for Census 2027 are progressing, with 23 states and Union Territories completing this initial phase. Himachal Pradesh recently began its field operations, while Kerala and Nagaland launched self-enumeration. This digital-first census aims for comprehensive coverage, collecting data on housing and household details through a structured questionnaire.

title: JCO, 3 soldiers hurt in accidental blast along LoC in Jammu & Kashmir’s Rajouri
content: Four Army personnel, including a JCO and three jawans, were injured in an accidental landmine blast during a patrol near the LoC in Rajouri district. The explosion occurred during an area domination exercise in a forward area, with displaced mines due to rain cited as a potential cause.

title: 329 million children in India exposed to extreme heat: UNICEF report
content: A UN report reveals 92% of Indian children face extreme heat, with 89 million exposed to heatwaves. While India has Heat Action Plans, they lack child-specific measures. India's high hazard exposure score, especially for extreme heat and air pollution, highlights the urgent need for targeted interventions to protect young lives.

title: 'If everyone in this country ... ': CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke clears stance on fighting polls
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke ruled out electoral politics, emphasizing that citizens shouldn't need to contest elections to demand basic rights. Ahead of a nationwide demonstration against the NEET-UG paper leak, Dipke criticized the Centre for its lack of engagement with the youth and demanded the Union Education Minister's resignation.

title: US used Iran-style covert transfers to move 90 million barrels of oil out of Gulf: Report
content: The U.S. has reportedly established a covert offshore oil-transfer network near the Strait of Hormuz, mirroring Iran's tactics to maintain Gulf crude exports. This operation, involving ship-to-ship transfers off Oman and the UAE, aims to circumvent Tehran's blockade of the vital waterway. While enabling continued exports, the network's reliance on clandestine methods raises concerns about its vulnerability and the U.S.

title: ‘Weapon more powerful than any nuke’: Iran can shut Strait of Hormuz again, US intel warns
content: Iran now possesses the capability to effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil and gas route, according to US intelligence. This development comes despite an upcoming peace agreement aimed at reopening the waterway. Concerns persist over Iran's military arsenal and its potential to use the strait as leverage in future disputes, even as diplomatic efforts continue.

title: 'Can always come to Moscow': Russia's open invitation to Zelenskyy for ‘serious’ dialogue
content: Ukraine's President Zelenskyy is welcome in Moscow for serious peace talks, the Kremlin stated, though no official communication channels exist. This comes amid renewed diplomatic efforts, including US President Trump's discussions with Zelenskyy and anticipated visits by American representatives to Russia. European leaders urged a firmer stance against Moscow at the G7 summit.

title: Call drops, frozen payments and buffering screens are the new traffic jams of India's cities
content: A TRAI assessment reveals inconsistent mobile network performance on Delhi-NCR's Metro and Namo Bharat corridors, impacting commuters' daily digital lives. Despite advancements, seamless connectivity remains a challenge due to underground infrastructure and moving users, highlighting the growing need for integrated telecom solutions in urban transit.

title: 'Shameless': Oppn targets PM Modi for meeting Trump after US strikes killed 3 Indian sailors
content: In the wake of Prime Minister Modi's encounter with Donald Trump at the G7, opposition factions have come out strongly against him, highlighting the heartbreaking deaths of three Indians due to a US military strike. The Congress party circulated a video purportedly showing Trump dismissing Modi, and the AAP denounced the cordiality of their interaction as 'embarrassing.

title: Amin Hajee recalls career struggles after Lagaan and how Aamir Khan helped him recover
content: A quarter century after Lagaan became one of Indian cinema's most celebrated films, actor and filmmaker Amin Hajee has made a candid admission about the impact the movie had on his career. While the Aamir Khan-starrer opened doors for many associated with it, Hajee says it "destroyed" him in an unexpected way. Hajee revealed that his wife Charlotte was concerned about him sitting at home and turning down projects after Lagaan.

title: How Qatar, not Pakistan, became the real power broker in US-Iran deal
content: While Pakistan sought the spotlight in its diplomatic endeavours, it was Qatar’s subtle, low-key mediation that ultimately held the key to the US-Iran ceasefire. Leveraging its unique relationship with Tehran and access to the American administration, Qatar's patient and discreet shuttle diplomacy triumphed over more boisterous efforts that were ridiculed globally.

title: Cues from Lucknow nets: Rohit faces chin music, Iyer rehearses the pull
content: Rohit Sharma, Shubman Gill, Shreyas Iyer and Ishan Kishan put in the hard yards at the Ekana Stadium ahead of the second ODI against Afghanistan. While Kuldeep Yadav bowled an extended spell, Iyer worked extensively on the pull shot that proved to be his undoing in Dharamsala. India will take on Afghanistan on Wednesday in Lucknow.

title: Watch: PM Modi and Donald Trump exchange greetings at G7 Summit ahead of expected talks
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump had a brief interaction at the G7 Summit in France, their first in 16 months. The leaders were seen shaking hands and engaging in conversation, with Trump also patting Modi's arm. They were seated next to each other during a summit session.

title: Water crisis deepens in Mumbai: BMC halts supply to pools, construction sites
content: Mumbai faces severe water scarcity as lake levels drop to 10.35%, prompting the BMC to impose a 20% cut on industrial and commercial supply, suspend water to construction sites and swimming pools, and limit bottling plants. These measures, effective June 17, add to existing citywide restrictions amid a delayed monsoon.

title: Rupee snaps two-day rally, settles 2 paise lower at 94.60 against US dollar
content: The rupee dipped slightly to 94.60 against the dollar on Tuesday, ending a two-day gain. Despite falling crude oil prices and hopes for a US-Iran peace deal, foreign investors selling Indian equities pressured the currency. Analysts anticipate a near-term downward trend for the rupee, with support from easing oil prices.

title: 'Huge plus for any team': Sunil Gavaskar identifies India's next X-factor
content: Nitish Kumar Reddy's impressive bowling, particularly a match-turning yorker to dismiss Rahmanullah Gurbaz, has earned high praise from Sunil Gavaskar.  The all-rounder's developing bowling prowess and batting ability offer significant tactical flexibility to the Indian ODI team, making him a valuable asset. His recent IPL success further solidifies his all-round credentials.

title: Pakistan-linked terror-crime network busted in Delhi-NCR, seven held
content: Delhi Police has apprehended seven individuals in connection with a Pakistan-backed terror-crime module. The operatives are accused of smuggling arms and narcotics from Pakistan, conducting reconnaissance of potential targets, and sharing information with handlers across the border. The network allegedly operated under the patronage of Pakistan's ISI.

title: 3 BLO visits, one missed form, voter list trouble? Telangana SIR puts urban voters on alert
content: Telangana is undertaking a Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls. Booth-level officers are tasked with visiting every household to verify voter status. Challenges like locked houses and migrant populations may lead to voters being marked absent. Eligible voters can seek inclusion during the claims and objections period. Political parties are urging vigilance to prevent the deletion of genuine voters.

title: AMCA and the future of air warfare: What it takes to build a fifth-generation fighter
content: India has launched the next phase of its Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) program, aiming for its first indigenous fifth-generation stealth fighter. This ambitious project, led by private industry, seeks to close the stealth gap with China and Pakistan, marking a significant shift in India's defense manufacturing.

title: Tamil Nadu’s own-tax effort has ‘collapsed’, says White Paper
content: Giving an account of how T.N.’s own tax revenue fared vis-à-vis 3 ‘peer States’ in last 5 years, the report says the SOTR-to-GSDP ratio declined from 5.93% to 5.45%, the ‘lowest in the State’s history and the steepest decline’ among the benchmarked States

title: Supreme Court steps in to ensure ‘dignified life’ for octogenarian, visually impaired son
content: The top court takes suo motu cognisance of newspaper reports highlighting the dire conditions in which they are living

title: Air Force ferrying NEET question papers by aircraft
content: Bundles flown to more than 20 locations across the country; papers delivered to several northern States, while dry runs were conducted at selected locations across southern India

title: Senior BJP leaders hold meeting, discuss organisational restructuring
content: Party discusses regional in-charges, expansion of office-bearers and organisational changes ahead of new national team and possible Cabinet reshuffle

title: Delhi Police bust Pakistan-backed terror syndicate, seven held
content: Police say the network was functioning under the patronage of Pakistan’s ISI and was using Indians to further terror activities; police have arrested suspects from Uttar Pradesh and Punjab

title: Supreme Court seeks Centre, States’ response on plea over ‘misuse’ of Aadhaar as proof of citizenship
content: The petition claims that ‘infiltrators and illegal immigrants’ are able to obtain Aadhaar cards and project themselves as ‘lawful residents’

title: Empty rations and silent radios: the death of an Indian seafarer onboard Celestial Sea
content: The vessel’s problems encompass almost everything that Indian seafarers have faced in the last two-and-a-half months: Depleting supplies, problems with shipowners, distress calls not being attended to, rationing of food and water, long stressful days in the war zone, and the U.S. Navy’s hostile manoeuvres.

title: Over-the-counter sale of cough syrup barred
content: Following the Drugs Rules amendment, lozenges, pills, and tablets for cough will still be available over the counter; 24 children in M.P. died after consuming adulterated cough syrup last year

title: Vijayabaskar quits as MLA, leaves AIADMK
content: He is the fifth AIADMK legislator to resign from the Assembly weeks after the Assembly elections in 2026.

title: Congress Ministers hit back at DMK after facing persistent criticism for joining TVK-led govt.
content: Their joint statement says the DMK must accept the people’s verdict and continue its work as a responsible opposition party

title: India’s rainfall deficit at 35% as monsoon progress stalls
content: Central India faces 63% deficit as monsoon’s first round loses momentum; govt. directs crop-wise contingency plans, puts around 200 districts under priority monitoring, orders weekly reviews

title: PM Modi holds bilateral talks with leaders of U.K., UAE on the sidelines of G7 Summit
content: The Modi-Starmer meeting came at a time when the U.K. Prime Minister’s political future is in question – a byelection could put a potential contender for his post, Andy Burnham, back in the U.K. Parliament.

title: Maharashtra Govt to set up medical cyclotron project in Nagpur to elevate cancer research
content: The goal is to ensure local availability of radioisotopes required for PET-CT and Nuclear Med services.

title: Woman held for death of boy in Vellore
content: The police said the arrested person was identified as a native of Melpatti village and aunt of the victim

title: NEET-UG re-exam: Telegram app restricted in India at NTA request
content: The agency said that several Telegram groups were advertising fake exam papers for the upcoming NEET exam and defrauding candidates willing to pay for them

title: Outreach to high-risk population, standardized, intelligence-led response model must to nullify cyber fraud challenges: U.P. police
content: “Given that AI voice and video spoofing is expected to escalate future risks, it is essential for individuals to conduct independent verification by contacting official police and bank helplines”

title: Is Lok Sabha Speaker opposed to Rahul Gandhi’s Kota visit, asks Ashok Gehlot
content: He said the functionaries linked to the office of Lok Sabha Speaker were pressuring coaching institutes, paying guest accommodations and guesthouse operators to discourage students from attending the programme

title: Six persons held for selling baby in Tirupattur
content: The police said Shabbir Ahmed, a person with disability and husband of Jabina who was also arrested, learnt about the sale and alerted local police

title: World suffers from shortage of trust: PM Modi to G7 leaders
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump greet each other against a backdrop of strained India-U.S. ties; the two leaders are scheduled to meet on Wednesday

title: After residents’ petition to Collector, six TASMAC outlets in Vellore, Ranipet and Tirupattur to be relocated
content: After a consultative meeting with local police and revenue officials on alternative accommodation of identified outlets by the District Collector, these outlets will be shifted. Paperwork for the relocation has begun

title: Record surge in women patronage in KSRTC on Priyadarshini scheme’s inaugural day
content: On June 15, the KSRTC saw 13.29 lakh women passengers use its services, of which zero-fare tickets were issued to 7.83 lakh women. The KSRTC incurs a revenue loss of ₹2.20 crore on the first day following the launch of the scheme.

title: UP, Gujarat, Jharkhand, 10 other States record revenue surplus in FY25: CAG report
content: Of the 18 States that targeted revenue surplus, nine achieved the target, while Assam, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Mizoram, and Telangana ended up being revenue deficit in 2024-25.

title: G7 Summit: Modi holds talks with Canada’s Carney, aims to visit Canada by year end, sign trade deal soon
content: The two countries have been negotiating a free trade agreement, with Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal visiting Canada last month with a delegation of over 100 industry representatives

title: Tripura Congress lodges complaint against CRPF over ‘assault of youth Congress members’
content: Senior party leaders have also demanded action against the CRPF jawans who allegedly used abusive language against the workers at a NEET protest rally

title: TMC MP Saugata Roy threatens breach of privilege motion against rebel MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar
content: Mr. Roy accuses Ms. Dastidar of breaking rules after she discussed Parliamentary standing committee meet with media; she blamed former TMC government for rampant child trafficking, marriage in W.B.

title: Participation in Yoga Day made mandatory for West Bengal Govt. staff
content: This will be the PM’s first public appearance in Bengal after he took part in the CM’s swearing in ceremony

title: West Bengal CID grills Abhishek Banerjee for third consecutive day
content: The Trinamool national general secretary is questioned in connection with an FIR lodged at the Bidhannagar police station for allegedly making provocative remarks in some speeches posted on social media

title: SC issues notice on plea seeking oversight of rehabilitation centres catering to children with disabilities
content: Observing that the matter raises important issues, the Bench said it required consideration “on priority”

title: IAF to participate in Exercise Pitch Black 2026 in Australia
content: Australian High Commissioner says the exercise is an opportunity to strengthen regional interoperability across the Indo-Pacific

title: Hindu prayers made mandatory in Chhattisgarh’s State schools; govt imposing RSS agenda, says Congress
content: Morning assembly will include the national anthem, national song, Deep Mantra, Saraswati Vandana, and Guru Mantra

title: TVK government’s White Paper puts Tamil Nadu’s debt at ₹13.18 lakh crore
content: The report says the headline debt figure of ₹10 lakh crore in 2025-26 captures only the State’s direct borrowings through market loans, institutional debt, and public account liabilities; it does not capture the borrowings of the PSUs, statutory boards, and special purpose vehicles that the State guarantees or implicitly supports

title: Indian Ocean Rim Association ‘examines’ Canada’s application to become observer
content: Sanjiv Ranjan, IORA’s Secretary-General, said member states could benefit from Canada’s inclusion into the fold considering its expertise in varied maritime domains.

title: Teachers’ Recruitment Board to release mark list for filling 2,708 assistant professor posts by end of June
content: It is now awaiting clarification from the Higher Education Department on whether it can proceed with filling 1,292 additional assistant professor posts after the HC cleared the decks for it last month

title: After nine years, two BSF men handed 20-year prison terms in Mizoram rape-acid attack case
content: The BSF personnel threw acid on one of the victims, disfiguring her face and causing partial loss of vision

title: LIVE Updates: US-Iran Deal To Be Signed In Switzerland On Friday
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Netanyahu pledged that Iran would never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons, regardless of the terms of any agreement.

title: US-Iran Deal To Be Signed In Switzerland On Friday
content: A US-Iran deal aimed at ending the Middle East war will be signed at Switzerland's mountainside Burgenstock resort on Friday, the Swiss foreign ministry said.

title: Iran Threatens "Harsh Response" To Israeli Attacks In Lebanon
content: Iran's military threatened on Tuesday to respond to Israel after strikes in southern Lebanon killed four people despite a deal between Tehran and Washington ending the Middle East war, including in Lebanon.

title: 'Bharat Innovates' To Be Annual Event? Harish Salve Exclusive
content: Harish Salve said the deals that were a spin-off from the Bharat Innovates programme, while not worth multi-billion dollars, can still be the "building blocks for the future of India".

title: Protest Held In Dhaka Over Opposition To Construction Of Lord Ram Statue
content: Minority groups in Bangladesh are demanding action against the threats of a project to build the country's largest statue of Lord Ram in Gaibandha's Palashbari upazila.

title: "There Would Be No Israel Without Me": Trump Amid Netanyahu Rift Buzz
content: Trump's comments came as he urged Netanyahu to be more cautious over Israel's strikes in Lebanon, which he said could affect efforts towards a peace deal with Iran.

title: G7 Leaders Agree To Push Russia To End Ukraine War
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky joined the summit at the French resort of Evian-les-Bains, with Ukraine seen as holding up well on the battlefield but its cities still the target of deadly Russian attacks.

title: SpaceX Overtakes Amazon To Become World's 5th Most Valuable Company
content: In a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission, SpaceX said the all-stock deal was expected to close in the third quarter of this year and that Cursor would become a wholly owned subsidiary.

title: Phones Off, Uddhav Sena MPs Not Reachable, Focus Now Shifts To Delhi
content: Uddhav Thackeray and other senior party leaders are making personal efforts to persuade the MPs. Some parliamentarians have been described as "not reachable".

title: Man Assaults Wife, Then Chases Her Down, Kills Her On Way To Police Station
content: The victim, identified as Nisha Jatav, was attacked with an iron rod near an agricultural field in Sahariyan Ka Pura village under the Dimni police station limits on Monday evening.

title: Russian Artist Critical Of Putin Shot Dead In Poland, 2 Belarusians Arrested
content: According to Polish officials, Skrepetsky -- whose real name is Robert Kuzovkov -- was shot three times on Monday morning by an unidentified gunman armed with a handgun.

title: India-UK Trade Deal Takes Centre Stage As PM Modi Meets Starmer At G7 Summit
content: The meeting came against the backdrop of strengthening ties between the two countries following the reciprocal visits by the two leaders last year, which injected fresh impetus into the India-UK Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.

title: Devrishi Narad Awards Honour Excellence In Journalism Across 12 Categories
content: The awards recognised excellence in journalism across 12 categories, celebrating contributions ranging from rural reporting and women's issues to digital journalism and content creation.

title: 4 Meets In 12 Days: Congress Scrambles To End Punjab Infighting Before Polls
content: The Congress central leadership has failed to resolve the infighting within the Punjab Congress cadre. Now it is taking feedback from MPs, MLAs and grassroot leadership to resolve the matter.

title: PM Modi, Carney Meet At G7: India, Canada Set 2026 Deadline For Trade Deal
content: The Evian meeting builds on the groundwork laid during Carney's visit to India in March, increasingly being read as the inflexion point of the bilateral reset.

title: Exam Irregularities Continue Despite Testing Body Reforms: Parliamentary Panel
content: The committee, headed by Digvijaya Singh, noted that such incidents had led to examination cancellations and caused significant stress for students preparing for highly competitive tests.

title: Bengaluru Horror: Instagram Friendship, Live-In Relationship, Then Murder
content: The couple had reportedly met through Instagram and had been living together in Malleswaram for the past six months.

title: Pension Delayed For 3 Months, Odisha Woman Consumes Pesticide, Dies
content: The daughter of the victim alleged that her mother had been repeatedly visiting the bank to check whether the pension amount had been credited.

title: Indian H-1B Worker Sues US Employer For Demanding Rs 94 Lakh From Him
content: The worker, identified as Rishikesh Raj Meesala, alleged that he was pressured into making payments by his Indian American employer Sai Jitender Kalagra.

title: Ritabrata says he has support of 65 Trinamool MLAs, dares rivals to take floor test
content: MLAs backing Mamata Banerjee not invited to the Business Advisory Committee meeting convened  by Speaker

title: ‘Not me’: Punjab CM denies that he is the person in a video that provoked the wrath of the Akal Takht
content: Bhagwant Mann claims he is the victim of “a malicious and politically motivated conspiracy” by SAD; Congress slams his confrontational response; BJP says he has no moral legitimacy to continue as CM

title: NEET leak accused gets court nod to take retest
content: Koushiki Saha, a trainee journalist currently reporting for The Times of India, covers urban governance, infrastructure lapses, public grievances, and municipal policies with clarity and compassion. Still learning every day, she draws insights from fieldwork, lived experiences, and holding authorities accountable through persistent, people-focused reporting. Read More

title: TMC, Sena (UBT) flux: Bid to boost NDA numbers in Lok Sabha for women's bill?
content: govt can bring back its bill to roll out quota for women in Lok Sabha and assemblies from 2029, with nearly 50% rise in seats of legislatures expected in parliament's monsoon session . a top BJP functionary asserts that the bill will be brought in the coming session, a ally says .

title: Lower oil prices to offer relief to consumers, government
content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: PM Modi meets Trump after 16 months, with a handshake, not a hug
content: a formal bilateral is expected on the margins of the summit in the resort town of Evian on friday . the two leaders reaffirmed their commitment to concluding the negotiations for a comprehensive economic partnership agreement at an early date .

title: Uddhav’s MPs landing in Delhi today to effect a split: Reports
content: there were reports that 6 of the 9 LS MPs could reach New Delhi on Wednesday . there was speculation that the 6 could form a separate group by giving a letter to LS Speaker Om Birla in the next few days .

title: Tughlaqabad fire toll rises to 4; man dies in Alipur car blaze
content: the death toll in the building fire in southeast Delhi’s Tughlaqabad Extension has risen to four . the deceased, Guddi Devi, lived on the third floor of the building . her son Pankaj Kumar Pandey, daughter Soni and mother Sushila Devi had earlier died in the incident .

title: To Tehran with love? Trump's Iran MoU meets MAGA mutiny
content: he pledges to hold a detailed press conference after friday's formal signing ceremony . critics savaged him for a deal that the white house has promoted as a landmark achievement ending months of conflict with Tehran . the moU, signed electronically this week, is due to be formalised in Switzerland on Friday .
